_meta.source_policy: PDF-derived learning content; story structure and characters are model_inference.

scientific_core_question.label: model_inference
scientific_core_question.text: When can a quantum-information claim survive contact with the physical conditions of quantum optics: basis choice, measurement, approximations, dissipation, and implementation limits?

emotional_core.label: model_inference
emotional_core.text: A first-year protagonist refuses to pretend she understands; the team learns that careful questions about assumptions can matter more than fast recitation.

previous_failure_connection.label: model_inference
previous_failure_connection.safe_summary: Last year's team lost after giving a brilliant calculation without stating the regime in which it was valid.

inheritance_question.label: model_inference
inheritance_question.text: The senior passes down not an answer, but a habit: name the space, name the approximation, and name what would break it.

final_conflict.label: model_inference
final_conflict.safe_summary: The final session asks the team to synthesize quantum-state language, light-matter dynamics, decoherence, and implementation requirements without overclaiming.
final_conflict.spoiler_control: Final result and decisive dialogue are sealed.

possible_scientific_turning_points.label: source_pdf
possible_scientific_turning_points.text: A reduced density operator is needed when a subsystem is entangled with an unobserved system.

possible_scientific_turning_points.label: source_pdf
possible_scientific_turning_points.text: Coherent exchange in a cavity must be compared against damping and spontaneous decay.

possible_scientific_turning_points.label: source_pdf
possible_scientific_turning_points.text: No-cloning and measurement limits change what counts as information processing.

possible_scientific_turning_points.label: source_pdf
possible_scientific_turning_points.text: Implementation claims must satisfy physical requirements, not just circuit diagrams.
